visit_tasks_note:
* cleaned kitchen 
* Cleaned bathrooms 
* Put washing
* Put washing out and once finished hung out
* Rang Telstra on Trish’s behalf as she could not ring out on her phone. Telstra claimed she had not paid the bill but I took Trish to post office on the 13 Nov. 
* Telstra said that money went on an old account as they have up dated their billing / payment system so she will get a cheque in the mail. 
* Trish was to pay the bill again today and the have given her phone access to calling out again.
* I explained that  for Trish to not be able to call out was not acceptable as she may have needed help from a family member not an ambulance.
* She is able to call out now as well tested it.
* I fell I’d like to call the ombudsman about this as  it is not good for Telstra to cut service over one bill unpaid.
membership_community_names: NSW - Central Coast, NSW - CM - Jannayah Alderdice, State - NSW

visit_rate: Assistance with self-care and activities of daily living - Standard
visit_tasks_note:
Upon arrival, the client appeared surprised and stated she was not aware that support was scheduled for today. She presented as visibly upset and reported experiencing ongoing family issues, which had left her feeling down and unmotivated.

The client declined a shower, advising she had showered yesterday and did not feel up to it today. She expressed feeling hopeless and low in energy.

I remained with the client for the duration of the scheduled hour to provide emotional support and conversation. Throughout our discussion, the client’s mood appeared to improve. She stated that talking helped her feel much better and happier, and expressed that she “just needed someone to talk to.”

No further concerns noted at the end of the shift.
membership_community_names: NSW - Central Coast, NSW - CM - Jannayah Alderdice, State - NSW

visit_tasks_note:
Today I met the lovely Peter, he has been unwell for sometime with mental health issues.  Please note you need to be careful with patient s and understanding. He has a book he uses for his everyday tasks and refers to it  Peter suffers anxiety and OCD.  A very independent person that sometimes needs reminding or prompting for things he needs to focus on. 

Today he requested shopping at Erina at Woolworths for groceries. 
Completing a form for Lumia Care, 
Going  to his bank for cash, he had  lost his visa and wallet at home. Peter had a bank statement to refer to and received his cash.
We visited Tea2 for his favourite tea then enjoyed a coffee together.

I feel Peter was a little overwhelmed in the beginning although he warmed up knowing he had a support person with him.  I called the office requesting a longer time to complete his tasks. 
I then returned him home , brought his bags into the house then left.
membership_community_names: NSW - Central Coast, NSW - CM - Jannayah Alderdice, State - NSW

Besides being tired, John appeared in good spirits.

visit_tasks_note:

visit_tasks_note:
Karan was attended this morning for a routine skin integrity and wellbeing check. She was alert, calm, and cooperative throughout the visit. Moisturiser was applied to both lower legs to maintain skin hydration. On assessment, the skin appeared intact with no signs of redness, oedema, or breakdown. No open wounds were present, and overall skin condition appeared healthy.

Karan reported ongoing pain, which she identified as consistent with sciatic nerve discomfort. She mentioned that she is awaiting a GP appointment for further evaluation and management. 

Vital signs were recorded as BP 143/82 mmHg, pulse 70 bpm, and SpO₂ 97%. She tolerated the visit well and expressed satisfaction with the care provided.

I brought a notebook with me and we are going to slowly get together a family tree as he remembers names on our visits.
